## Thread: Help with programming an exercise. — 3 msgs

_Topics: [`Help requests and how-to`](topics.md#help)_

### Help with programming an exercise.

- **From:** alivekkng@aol.com (AliveKkng)
- **Date:** 1999-04-27T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<19990426221810.01999.00001688@ng61.aol.com>`

```
Cobol code for:

Table Lookup - Single Dimensional Tables

From the student records illustrated below a departmental enrollment enrollment
analysis report is to be produced.  This is to be a group printed report
listing total departmental current hours enrolled.  The department name is to
be printed on the report.  The department numbers and related names are given
below.

    DEPARTMENT NUMBER                                   NAME

                   01                                                    
Accounting
                   02                                                    
Bamlomg
                   03                                                    
Business
                   04                                                    
Computer Science
                   05                                                    
Economics
                   06                                                    
Marketing
                   07                                                    
Management
                   08                                                     Real
Estate
                   09                                                    
Savings and Loan
                   10                                                    
Traffic

INPUT:  Student Records  (a:\abc.dat)
    3-4                         department number
    5-7                          student number
    8-27                        student name
    30-38                      social security number
    40-42                      hours earned to date
    50-52                      hours currently enrolled

OUTPUT:  Departmental Enrollment Analysis Report

                                   ENROLLMENT ANALYSIS REPORT
                                                 April 1, 1999

        DEPT                      DEPARTMENT                                   
   TOTAL
         NO.                             NAME                                  
      ENROLLMENTS  
         XX                         XXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXX                  ZZZZO
         XX                         XXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXX                  ZZZZ9

                                                                  FINAL TOTAL  
          ZZZZZ9

PROGRAM BY Desperate in the Mid-West
```

```
Here's the exercise:

Redo the following exercise . . .

Cobol code for:

Table Lookup - Single Dimensional Tables

From the student records illustrated below a departmental enrollment enrollment
analysis report is to be produced.  This is to be a group printed report
listing total departmental current hours enrolled.  The department name is to
be printed on the report.  The department numbers and related names are given
below.

    DEPARTMENT NUMBER                                   NAME

                   01                                                    
Accounting
                   02                                                    
Bamlomg
                   03                                                    
Business
                   04                                                    
Computer Science
                   05                                                    
Economics
                   06                                                    
Marketing
                   07                                                    
Management
                   08                                                     Real
Estate
                   09                                                    
Savings and Loan
                   10                                                    
Traffic

INPUT:  Student Records  (a:\abc.dat)
    3-4                         department number
    5-7                          student number
    8-27                        student name
    30-38                      social security number
    40-42                      hours earned to date
    50-52                      hours currently enrolled

OUTPUT:  Departmental Enrollment Analysis Report

                                   ENROLLMENT ANALYSIS REPORT
                                                 April 1, 1999

        DEPT                      DEPARTMENT                                   
   TOTAL
         NO.                             NAME                                  
      ENROLLMENTS  
         XX                         XXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXX                  ZZZZO
         XX                         XXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXX                  ZZZZ9

                                                                  FINAL TOTAL  
          ZZZZZ9

PROGRAM BY: Desperate in the Mid-West


. . . to create a detailed, control break report as indicated below.  The data
will need to be sorted into department number sequence first, and then used to
create the desired i\output.

INPUT:  Student Records  (a:\abc.dat)
    3-4                         department number
    5-7                          student number
    8-27                        student name
    30-38                      social security number
    40-42                      hours earned to date
    50-52                      hours currently enrolled


OUTPUT:

                                    ENROLLMENT ANALYSIS REPORT            PAGE 
01

    DEPT                 STUDENT NAME                     HOURS CURRENTLY
     NO.                                                                      
ENROLLED

      01                    JOE MURRY                                        12
      01                    SANDY WILSON                                  15

                                             ACCOUNTING TOTAL             27

      02                     MILLIE SMITH                                      
  5
      02                     JAMES JULIAN                                      
8

                                             BANKING TOTAL                     
13

                                                  FINAL TOTAL                  
   ZZ9

PROGRAM BY:Desperate in the Mid-West


NOTE:  Cannot use the COBOL SORT feature.




SOMEONE PLEASE HELP ME!!!
```

#### ↳ Re: Would someone please see if they can help me? COBOL Tables

- **From:** alowishus@my-dejanews.com
- **Date:** 1999-04-27T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol

- **References:** `<19990426223635.01999.00001705@ng61.aol.com>`

```


> NOTE:  Cannot use the COBOL SORT feature.

I am not sure if this mean you are not to sort the file.  So right off the
top of my head i would maybe search the file for department one's and print
them as I find them.  Then when EOF print totals and start the file again
this time for department 2's.  There probely is a better way to do it but I
would need to think about it.

I am assuming you don't know how many people can be in a department so you
can't do a nice array, and if you can't sort the records first.

Hope this helps

Will
```

#### ↳ Re: Would someone please see if they can help me? COBOL Tables

- **From:** "Warren Porter" <warrenp123@netdoor123.com>
- **Date:** 1999-04-27T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<s6lV2.1340$JC1.69950@axe.netdoor.com>`
- **References:** `<19990426223635.01999.00001705@ng61.aol.com>`

```

AliveKkng wrote in message <19990426223635.01999.00001705@ng61.aol.com>...
>Here's the exercise:
>
…[4 more quoted lines elided]…
>Table Lookup - Single Dimensional Tables
[snip for brevity]
>PROGRAM BY:Desperate in the Mid-West
>
>
>NOTE:  Cannot use the COBOL SORT feature.


If you can't use COBOL SORT, use an indexed file instead.  Open it output,
close it, open it I-O and write to it instead of sort RELEASE statements.
Close the file, open it for input, and start it with a low-value for the
key.  Instead of sort RETURN statements, do READ ... NEXT statements
instead.

You would define the file as DYNAMIC.  If there is any possibility of
duplicate records, add a sequence field as the last field in the key.  When
you are shutting down the program, after closing your indexed file use a
DELETE FILE statement on it.

HTH


---
Warren Porter -- Remove numbers to reply.

Y2K Sale -- Make no payments before 1900.
```

```
We have just converted our Micro Focus Cobol to Fujitsu Cobol about 6
months ago.  We are using Cobol SP2 for our GUI interface and
currently converting my client's financial system to Fujitsu Cobol
using Cobol SP2 thin client and Tuxedo on the Windows NT server. The
Windows NT C/S is connected to a Unisys Clearpath (2200) mainframe.
We are half way through and have not found any problems yet.

If you need more info email me or visit our site at www.mc-sys.com and
look at the screen shots of Fujitsu Cobol and Cobol SP2.

Tony M. Mina
```

<a id="t-5b7173ee89"></a>
## Thread: I am desperately seeking help!!! !!! !!! — 1 msgs

### I am desperately seeking help!!! !!! !!!

- **From:** alivekkng@aol.com (AliveKkng)
- **Date:** 1999-04-27T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<19990426222644.01999.00001700@ng61.aol.com>`

```
Here's the exercise.  Can anyone help?  I can help with Access programs.

Cobol code for:

Table Lookup - Single Dimensional Tables

From the student records illustrated below a departmental enrollment enrollment
analysis report is to be produced.  This is to be a group printed report
listing total departmental current hours enrolled.  The department name is to
be printed on the report.  The department numbers and related names are given
below.

    DEPARTMENT NUMBER                                   NAME

                   01                                                    
Accounting
                   02                                                    
Bamlomg
                   03                                                    
Business
                   04                                                    
Computer Science
                   05                                                    
Economics
                   06                                                    
Marketing
                   07                                                    
Management
                   08                                                     Real
Estate
                   09                                                    
Savings and Loan
                   10                                                    
Traffic

INPUT:  Student Records  (a:\abc.dat)
    3-4                         department number
    5-7                          student number
    8-27                        student name
    30-38                      social security number
    40-42                      hours earned to date
    50-52                      hours currently enrolled

OUTPUT:  Departmental Enrollment Analysis Report

                                   ENROLLMENT ANALYSIS REPORT
                                                 April 1, 1999

        DEPT                      DEPARTMENT                                   
   TOTAL
         NO.                             NAME                                  
      ENROLLMENTS  
         XX                         XXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXX                  ZZZZO
         XX                         XXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXX                  ZZZZ9

                                                                  FINAL TOTAL  
          ZZZZZ9

PROGRAM BY Desperate in the Mid-West
```

## Thread: On the seventh day he documented — 11 this month / 12 total

### On the seventh day he documented

- **From:** "Donald Tees" <donald@willmack.com>
- **Date:** 1999-04-28T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<7g7dds$c56$1@news.igs.net>`

```
While this a a bit off-topic, it is close enough that I think
this is the best place to post it.

I would like to start a discussion on documentation tools.
Like a lot of others in this NG, I have recently converted
a number of systems to Windows95(Fujitsu, in this case).
I now have to start in on new manuals and help systems,
and do not have a clue where to start.

I am thinking in terms of HTML documents, but would like
to hear what everybody is doing, and what tools they are
using.
```

#### ↳ Re: On the seventh day he documented

- **From:** emilne@my-dejanews.com
- **Date:** 1999-04-28T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<7g7ilh$i0t$1@nnrp1.dejanews.com>`
- **References:** `<7g7dds$c56$1@news.igs.net>`

```
In article <7g7dds$c56$1@news.igs.net>,
  "Donald Tees" <donald@willmack.com> wrote:
> I would like to start a discussion on documentation tools.
> Like a lot of others in this NG, I have recently converted
> a number of systems to Windows95(Fujitsu, in this case).
> I now have to start in on new manuals and help systems,
> and do not have a clue where to start.

You might want to check out Microsoft's HTM Workshop.  This has the
traditional appearance of a Windows Help window but uses HTML pages for
the detail information.  The Workshop is a utility that lets you create
and modify the Help file. It is a free download from Microsoft.  See
http://msdn.microsoft.com/workshop/author/htmlhelp/default.asp.

One thing to watch.  You may find that results only work with Internet
Explorer.

Ed Milne

----- Posted via Deja.com, The People-Powered Information Exchange -----
------ http://www.deja.com/   Discussions * Ratings * Communities ------
```

```
painter to create it - but they have to have IE and/or Windows 98 to
view it.  The .HLP files are upwardly compatible.

end users.  I'm anxious to see the discussion that develops.
```

#### ↳ Re: On the seventh day he documented

- **From:** tim.oxler@NOSPAMteo-computer.com (Tim Oxler)
- **Date:** 1999-04-29T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<37286238.4690573@news.teo-computer.com>`

```
On Wed, 28 Apr 1999 12:39:20 -0400, "Donald Tees"
<donald@willmack.com> wrote:

>While this a a bit off-topic, it is close enough that I think
>this is the best place to post it.
…[11 more quoted lines elided]…
>


You might consider writing them in Acrobat.  Acrobat Reader is
freeware that can be distributed with the documentation.  The Acrobat
editor is more expensive than Word and not extensively distributed,
like Word and it's available HTML editor and translation to document
capabilities.

This way, you reduce the likelihood of someone changing the
documentation, and having multiple versions.



Tim Oxler
Teo Computer
http://www.teo-computer.com
Take the Y2k Quiz
http://www.teo-computer.com/dev/quiz.html
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: On the seventh day he documented

- **From:** Ken Foskey <waratah@zip.com.au>
- **Date:** 1999-04-30T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3728E879.336C764C@zip.com.au>`
- **References:** `<7g7dds$c56$1@news.igs.net> <37286238.4690573@news.teo-computer.com>`

```
Tim Oxler wrote:
> 
> This way, you reduce the likelihood of someone changing the
> documentation, and having multiple versions.
> 

What you don't want others fixing up you documentation :->

I tried writing Word documents with links and saving as HTML.  In
a word don't it really does not work properly.

Personally here are my tips for HTML.

Keep it simple!   Some fo the pages I have seen built by tools are
twice as large as they need to be (literally).  Use basic HTML
commands and keep it very simple, if you have used ROFF or SCRIPT
(from the MVS mainframe) it really is not that bad.  The doco is
available for nothing from W3C.

I have seen a product similar to robo help used (can't remember
which one but it was very similar).  The results were very pretty
but there was a list a mile long on do's and don'ts, I don't know
whether our quality guys wanted a specific look and feel and
therefore imposed rules.

Watching this with interest....
Ken
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: On the seventh day he documented

- **From:** "Donald Tees" <donald@willmack.com>
- **Date:** 1999-04-30T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<7gcjs2$ja6$1@news.igs.net>`
- **References:** `<7g7dds$c56$1@news.igs.net> <37286238.4690573@news.teo-computer.com> <3728E879.336C764C@zip.com.au>`

```
What I sort of have fluttering around in my head is a picture
of hundreds of small documents being cross referenced by a data
base that generates the links.

In essence, current manuals could be deconstructed into modules that
go with program modules, and new modules written for individual
objects.  The cross-referencing would be kept separate, and generated
much like a program.
```

<a id="t-4fc212e793"></a>
## Thread: files > 2GB — 6 this month / 8 total

### files > 2GB

- **From:** "Vis" <kenka@polbox.com>
- **Date:** 1999-04-28T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<FlzV2.18607$811.359478@news.tpnet.pl>`

```
Hi,

Size of indexed files is limited to 2GB. How exceed this limit?
I use MicroFocus Cobol ver. 3.2 on unix.

regards

Wieslaw Sawicki
kenka@polbox.com
```

#### ↳ Re: files > 2GB

- **From:** "Rick Price" <rick@hpdi.demon.co.uk>
- **Date:** 1999-04-28T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<925288717.18864.0.nnrp-06.9e98b131@news.demon.co.uk>`
- **References:** `<FlzV2.18607$811.359478@news.tpnet.pl>`

```
This is a unix limit.   Later releases of unix have a 'different' file
system (I can't remember its proper name) which extends this but AFAIK
MicroFocus are not going to support this.

From MFCobol V4.1 they have something called file striping which allows a
file to be split into multiple files.  Unfortunately this is not automatic
and has to be planned for.  I haven't used it and it seems very clumsy to
me.

Be warned, later versions of MFCobol change the default locking method.
This means that shared index files start causing problems over 1GB.  This is
because the unix limit is 2GB on files opened exclusively but only 1 GB on
files opened shared.  To continue to use files > 1GB you need to set the
lock mode RTS tunable setting back to the V3 method for the runtime.

Regards
Rick

Vis wrote in message ...
>Hi,
>
…[9 more quoted lines elided]…
>
```

#### ↳ Re: files > 2GB

- **From:** "William M. Klein" <wmklein@nospam.netcom.com>
- **Date:** 1999-04-28T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<7g7ale$5qc@sjx-ixn10.ix.netcom.com>`
- **References:** `<FlzV2.18607$811.359478@news.tpnet.pl>`

```
3.2 is old enough that I don't remember for certain - but I *think* that it
does have a 2GB limit - that was lifted in one of the subsequent UNIX
versions (say one that came out about 1997 or so).
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: files > 2GB

- **From:** "Gazaloo" <gaz@home.com>
- **Date:** 1999-04-29T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<zoSV2.4397$gv5.2472@news.rdc1.sfba.home.com>`
- **References:** `<FlzV2.18607$811.359478@news.tpnet.pl> <7g7ale$5qc@sjx-ixn10.ix.netcom.com>`

```
i think the version to hunt for is 4.1 (it employs a striping tech to exceed
the UNIX file system limit -- i think it allows up to something like 200GB).

Gary.

ps. sorry Bill -- i sent this to you directly as well.

news:7g7ale$5qc@sjx-ixn10.ix.netcom.com...
> 3.2 is old enough that I don't remember for certain - but I *think* that
it
> does have a 2GB limit - that was lifted in one of the subsequent UNIX
> versions (say one that came out about 1997 or so).
…[19 more quoted lines elided]…
>
```

#### ↳ Re: files > 2GB

- **From:** "Darius Cooper" <Darius_cooper@Bigfoot.com>
- **Date:** 1999-04-29T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<zY8W2.1168$Sc5.4923@news.megsinet.net>`
- **References:** `<FlzV2.18607$811.359478@news.tpnet.pl>`

```
If your UNIX supports files > 2Gb (e.g. AIX ver 4.2)
and your file-system is set up to support files > 2GB
and your ulimit setting allows you to use files > 2GB
then you need the correct version of MF-COBOL.
On AIX, I think this is ver 4.1.20  with patch 13.10 applied...

- Darius Cooper

…[9 more quoted lines elided]…
```

- **From:** redsky@ibm.net (Thane Hubbell)
- **Date:** 1999-04-29T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3727abc7.288509586@news1.ibm.net>`
- **References:** `<37274161.3E433E66@skagit.ctc.edu>`

```
On Wed, 28 Apr 1999 10:12:02 -0700, Belinda Eggland
<Egglanbc@skagit.ctc.edu> wrote:

>Hi,
>I have a friend with a 386 Win. 3.11 machine, who is looking for a COBOL
>download.  Any ideas where I would find one that would run on a machine
>that old and slow?

You'll need 8 meg of Ram, but if you have it, the CD that is in the
book "Sams Teach Yourself COBOL in 24 Hours" includes the 16 bit
version of the Fujitsu COBOL compiler, and the book covers how to
install and use it.
(The book concentrates on the 32 bit version, but has sidebars that
explain any differenced between that and the 16 bit Windows 3.1
version).
```

```
implementation etc), identify cross-project dependencies and constraints and
impacts of any schedule or other change in order to manage and schedule
shared resources
- Enhance our current systems inventory with additional information

personnel with business and systems knowledge
- Establish a vehicle to manage project status at the master plan level
- Establish the policies and procedures for the PMO
- Develop and execute an implementation and communication strategy for the
PMO
- Transfer the management of the PMO to permanent personnel
- A Project Manager that is also interested in the permanent position will
be favourably considered for this position

REQUIREMENTS:
- Very strong knowledge of the systems development life cycle, with specific
knowledge to guide the development of the master plan identifying key
resource requirements such as test environment
- Knowledge of the technical environment that exists at the Board
- Deliverable focused approach in providing hands-on guidance and assistance
to the team towards meeting its objectives
- Proven record of successful project management experience
- Knowledge of the Navigator Project Management methodology is desirable but
not mandatory
- Solid foundation in project disciplines such as issue management, scope
management, status reporting, change management, project risks and quality
management
- Excellent facilitation, organization and communication skills
- Ability to develop professional presentations
- Proven record of establishing new business processes a definite asset
- Team building and conflict resolution skills
- Understanding of service quality principles and practices.
```

```
- Agree to follow all existing WCB standards, guidelines and conventions and
to author additions where and as required
- Provide project deliverables as defined by Navigator SDLC methodology
- Develop and maintain logistics plan/schedule
- Gather business and ISD logistics requirements
- Produce and acquire signoff of Service Level Agreement(s) with user
community and ISD development services and technical services staff
- Assist other staff as required to promote the system to the production

REQUIREMENTS
1. Project Leadership
- Proven experience in the implementation of similar sized development and
implementation efforts
2. Systems Analysis
- Proven experience in logistics: planning, procurement, rollout, support
- Experience with mainframe, and/or client/server and/or Internet logistics
3. General requirements
- Good working knowledge of SDLC concepts
- Ability to produce quality deliverables in a dynamic high-paced
environment
- Strong logistics and system analysis skills
- Proven
- People skills�: ability to work in a team environment and attain consensus
on business process and data changes
- Good communication skills (written, verbal, presentation, facilitation)
- Strong organization skills
- Extensive experience working in a windowing environment, suing word
processing and spreadsheet applications


Cindy, Recruiting Manager
OT & T Information Systems, INC
www.ottinf.com

Vancouver Branch:  Vancouver@ottinf.com
Tel: 604 271-8603
fax: 604 271-8203
~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
New York Branch: Newyork@ottinf.com
tel: 732 767-0940
Fax: 732 767-0940
```

<a id="t-e067141d9b"></a>
## Thread: Anyone else feel screwed by Fujitsu? — 8 this month / 13 total

_Topics: [`Compilers and vendors`](topics.md#compilers)_

### Anyone else feel screwed by Fujitsu?

- **From:** Fletch (fletcher.adams@eudoramail.com)
- **Date:** 1999-04-28T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<37285b7e.10289962@news-server>`

```
It's been about a month since everyone should have the 4.2 upgrade of
Fujitsu COBOL 4.0.

Am I the only one who doesn't?

I keep emailing, telling them I don't have it, and they keep telling
me it's been sent.

The latest is that it was sent FedEx Priority last week.  Here it is,
Tuesday evening, and I don't have as much as a tag on the door.

Why do they have to lie?  I mean, if they just don't want to honor the
year's service agreement I made with them, why don't they just say so?

Anyone else get screwed like this?
```

#### ↳ Re: Anyone else feel screwed by Fujitsu?

- **From:** "Jerry Peacock" <bismail@bisusa.com>
- **Date:** 1999-04-28T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<7g702b$o29$1@holly.prod.itd.earthlink.net>`
- **References:** `<37285b7e.10289962@news-server>`

```

Fletch wrote in message <37285b7e.10289962@news-server>...
<snip>
A similar thing happened re my local TELCO while rolling-out
its DSL offering. Demand - far exceeding estimates - has swamped
the company's ability to respond at the standard to which it normally
aspires.

OTOH, my dealings with Fujitsu have been exemplary (unlike those
rotten troglodytes at the phone company).
```

#### ↳ Re: Anyone else feel screwed by Fujitsu?

- **From:** "Gary Roush" <gkr@adtools.com>
- **Date:** 1999-04-28T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<_ePV2.979$uf6.95854@news20.ispnews.com>`
- **References:** `<37285b7e.10289962@news-server>`

```
Hey guys!

Have a little more backbone would ya? Those of you still ranting about it,
you might think about e-mailing Fujitsu AND showing your CURRENT mailing
address.  It turns out that several customers did not inform us about their
current addresses.  Several packages went to the old address.  Even some
had gone out by FedEx and guess what? Even then the address was wrong. The
person(s) simply did not tell us to check their address.  Perhaps we should
have asked them if this was their current address when talking over e-mail
or on the phone. The database is current with information as far as we
know. In fact one of them that posted in this newgroup on this thread had
just moved from one end to the end of the USA coast and had not informed us
of the new address AFTER calling us where his package was. Had he given us
the new address, the address database would have been updated and his
package sent to him as we had done. Well, it got signed by someone at that
OLD office place and now that package is stuck because it got signed.  Some
that were supposed to go airmail did not go out airmail.  Trust us, anyone
that did not receive one and should have will likely have a replacement
sent. Just be sure the CURRENT address exists in our database.

Now on another note, to have us be called liars or to think you'd been
screwed and mentioning it here not only hurts us, but hurts those of you
that are promoting Fujitsu COBOL as the COBOL compiler/runtime of the
future.  This be far from the truth that we would lie on purpose. If you
had been there in the shipping department, you would have seen all the work
that went into getting these 4.2 packages shipped worldwide from San Jose,
California, USA. The people doing the shipments did a darned good job the
best they could with what support they had.  Remember this was a major
release and probably the BIGGEST shipment ever experienced.

Also remember with your maintenance agreements being low-priced as they
are, you have quite a good deal in getting a good compiler with
improvements and the power that comes with it. You have unlimited support
available plus the patches and upgrades through the year. Now can anyone
tell me that there is another company that comes low-priced like this and
yet offers this much? I think not.

Now for those who are still waiting for their 4.2 compilers, I have a
suggestion. Please e-mail a NICE note and give your name (company name
included if it is a company), CURRENT address (include old/new address if
you moved), CURRENT phone number AND your serial number.

Send this to cobol@adtools.com  and use the subject "4.2 not received.
Please confirm shipment address."

Thank you for your cooperation and understanding.

Regards,
Gary Roush
Fujitsu COBOL Support

P.S.  These comments are personal and do not necessarily reflect Fujitsu
statements. Only that I have witnessed all the events of this happening.
This was a MAJOR release and we are going to handle it differently the next
time.

---------------------

Fletch wrote in message <37285b7e.10289962@news-server>...
>It's been about a month since everyone should have the 4.2 upgrade of
>Fujitsu COBOL 4.0.
…[12 more quoted lines elided]…
>Anyone else get screwed like this?
```

- **Date:** 1999-04-28T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3727cd3f@news3.us.ibm.net>`
- **References:** `<37285b7e.10289962@news-server> <_ePV2.979$uf6.95854@news20.ispnews.com>`


#### ↳ Re: Anyone else feel screwed by Fujitsu?

- **From:** News <news@siward.demon.co.uk>
- **Date:** 1999-04-28T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<CH1qEHAu8uJ3EwNF@siward.demon.co.uk>`
- **References:** `<37285b7e.10289962@news-server>`

```
In article <37285b7e.10289962@news-server>, Fletch <?@?.?> writes
>It's been about a month since everyone should have the 4.2 upgrade of
>Fujitsu COBOL 4.0.
>
>Am I the only one who doesn't?

[snip]

Here is a better one. I emailed Fujitsu about 2 weeks ago. No response.
Then I tried again yesterday and got a response that they had some
details about me on their database, but no record that I had purchased
V4.0 with support and they cannot locate the paper records about my
purchase - oh yes - they also did not receive my previous email. They
asked if I can send all the details that I sent last time. This is a bit
difficult at the moment because I am in Amsterdam now and the email was
sent from my home in London. So I resigned myself to having to wait
until I get back to London to resend the details.

Then, Lo! Another email asking me to ignore the previous email. This
email arrived perhaps 2 hours after the first. They have found the paper
record of my purchase, updated the database but, wait for it, cannot
send out the upgrade until I send them my serial number - despite having
registered online way back in August.

So, some response at last and I'm a bit closer, perhaps, to receiving
V4.2 once I have got home and emailed my serial number.

Hey ho - what a way to run a brewery!
```

<a id="t-8565ed9737"></a>
## Thread: cobol compilers — 3 msgs

_Topics: [`Compilers and vendors`](topics.md#compilers)_

### cobol compilers

- **From:** "George Jones" <grjiii@worldnet.att.net>
- **Date:** 1999-04-28T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<7g8ef8$ooh$1@bgtnsc03.worldnet.att.net>`

```
I have a need to be able to develop batch Cobol/DB2 applications on a
Windows '98 PC, then port to an IBM mainframe with as little modification as
possible.  I'd like to receive any information and/or hear about any
experiences with Cobol compilers and databases on the PC.  I'm currently
considering Microfocus with XDB, Fujitsu (with IBM DB2?), and possibly
Realia Cobol workbench, but I'd love to hear about these and any other
combinations of compilers and databases.  Thanks very much!

George R. Jones
Phoenix 2000 Incorporated
grjiii@att.net
```

#### ↳ Re: cobol compilers

- **From:** redsky@ibm.net (Thane Hubbell)
- **Date:** 1999-04-29T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3727cd09.1186893@news1.ibm.net>`
- **References:** `<7g8ef8$ooh$1@bgtnsc03.worldnet.att.net>`

```
On Wed, 28 Apr 1999 21:55:55 -0400, "George Jones"
<grjiii@worldnet.att.net> wrote:

>I have a need to be able to develop batch Cobol/DB2 applications on a
>Windows '98 PC, then port to an IBM mainframe with as little modification as
…[4 more quoted lines elided]…
>combinations of compilers and databases.  Thanks very much!

For the specific requirements here, I think you might best be served
with IBM's own Visual Age COBOL.  When bundled with DB2 its a pretty
good deal. 

If you were just targeting the PC, I'd say go with Fujitsu.
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: cobol compilers

- **From:** "William M. Klein" <wmklein@nospam.netcom.com>
- **Date:** 1999-04-29T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<7g8qik$ims@dfw-ixnews10.ix.netcom.com>`
- **References:** `<7g8ef8$ooh$1@bgtnsc03.worldnet.att.net> <3727cd09.1186893@news1.ibm.net>`

```
I second the "attached".  There are lots of times that other COBOL's might
be the right choice, but if you are doing PC development of BATCH COBOL/DB2
applications targeted for the IBM mainframe, using VisualAge for COBOL seems
the obvious (if not cheapest) choice.
```

<a id="t-e11576d5cf"></a>
## Thread: use of NULLs and compression issue — 1 msgs

### use of NULLs and compression issue

- **From:** lewandow@uwm.edu
- **Date:** 1999-04-28T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<7g6vkr$1o$1@nnrp1.dejanews.com>`

```
 I would like to know if anyone has experience dealing with compression
 algorithms.  We are trying to recode a program originally generated
by PRISM which performs compression on a PIC X(10) date field when
a NULL is inserted.  Currently the compression routine used by prism
is about 150 lines long - there must be a better way.  Following is
some pseudocode - sorry don't have access to the real thing from here.


05 RECEIVED-DATE   PIC X(10).
05 RECEIVED-INVOICE-NUMBER  PIC 9(10).
...

IF (RECEIVED-DATE = 0 OR RECEIVED-DATE = 9999999)
   MOVE 'NULL' TO RECEIVED-DATE
   PERFORM 1500-COMPRESSION
ELSE
   GOTO NEXT RECEIVED-DATE.
...
1500-COMPRESSION   SECTION.
   (here's where I get stuck)
   READ RECEIVED-DATE.
   MOVE-SPACES-FROM-DATE-TO-AFTER-RECEIVED-INVOICE-NUMBER.

Any ideas?  I'm a newbie working for a major retailer (new job).

KL


-----------== Posted via Deja News, The Discussion Network ==----------
http://www.dejanews.com/       Search, Read, Discuss, or Start Your Own
```

---

<a id="t-c796b0df69"></a>
## Thread: Problem with update program. — 7 this month / 10 total

### Problem with update program.

- **From:** jlmeyer@rmi.net (John Meyer)
- **Date:** 1999-04-29T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3728dc5c.7839242@news.rmi.net>`

```
Here are the relavant parts:

       ENVIRONMENT DIVISION.
       INPUT-OUTPUT SECTION.
           FILE-CONTROL.
               SELECT EMPLOYEE-MASTER
                   ASSIGN TO "C:\COBOL\EMPLISAM.DAT"
                   ORGANIZATION IS INDEXED
                   ACCESS IS RANDOM
                   RECORD KEY IS PM-EMPL-ID
                   STATUS IS I-O-STATUS.

               SELECT EMPLOYEE-TRANSACTION
                   ASSIGN TO "C:\COBOL\EMPLTRAN.DAT"
                   ORGANIZATION IS LINE SEQUENTIAL.

               SELECT PRINT-FILE
                   ASSIGN TO "C:\COBOL\PAYPRINT.TXT".
      *            ASSIGN TO PRINTER.

       01  I-O-STATUS                          PIC X(02).
           88  SUCCESSFUL-I-O                  VALUE IS '00'.
           88  READ-ERR-NOF                    VALUE IS '10'.
           88  ADD-ERR-DUPLICATE               VALUE IS '22'.


       301-DEL-TRANS-REC.
           INITIALIZE PAYROLL-MASTER
           MOVE ET-EMPLOYEE-NBR TO PM-EMPL-ID
           READ EMPLOYEE-MASTER RECORD
           IF SUCCESSFUL-I-O
               IF ET-LAST-NAME  = PM-LAST-NAME
                   PERFORM 350-PRINT-SUCCESSFUL-DELETE
                   DELETE EMPLOYEE-MASTER RECORD
               ELSE
                   PERFORM 351-PRINT-DEL-CORR-FAIL
               END-IF
           ELSE
               PERFORM 352-PRINT-DEL-NOF
           END-IF.



Problem:  This code will not evaluate a true "SUCCESSFUL-I-O".
```

```
	Okay, here is more of the code.

       ENVIRONMENT DIVISION.
       INPUT-OUTPUT SECTION.
           FILE-CONTROL.
               SELECT EMPLOYEE-MASTER
                   ASSIGN TO "C:\COBOL\emplisam.dat"
                   ORGANIZATION IS INDEXED

       DATA DIVISION.
       FILE SECTION.
       FD  EMPLOYEE-MASTER
           RECORD CONTAINS 98 CHARACTERS
           DATA RECORD IS PAYROLL-MASTER.
           COPY "C:\COBOL\PAYMST.REC".
       
       01  PAYROLL-MASTER.
           05  PM-EMPL-ID.
               10  PM-ID-1-3           PIC X(03).
               10  PM-ID-4-5           PIC X(02).
               10  PM-ID-6-9           PIC X(04).
           05  PM-EMPL-NAME.
               10  PM-LAST-NAME        PIC X(15).
               10  PM-FIRST-NAME       PIC X(15).
               10  PM-MIDDLE-INIT      PIC X(01).
           05  PM-STORE-ID             PIC X(01).
               88  HOME-STORE                      VALUE '1'.
               88  WAREHOUSE-STORE                 VALUE '2'.
               88  NORTH-STORE                     VALUE '3'.
               88  EAST-STORE                      VALUE '4'.
               88  WEST-STORE                      VALUE '5'.
               88  CENTRAL-STORE                   VALUE '6'.
           05  PM-DEPT-ID              PIC X(03).
               88  SALES-DEPT                      VALUE 'SLS'.
               88  ACCOUNTING-DEPT                 VALUE 'ACC'.
               88  INFO-SYS-DEPT                   VALUE 'IS'.
               88  WAREHOUSE-DEPT                  VALUE 'WHS'.
           05  PM-PAY-STATUS           PIC X(01).
               88  HOURLY-EMPL                     VALUE '2'.
               88  SALARY-EMPL                     VALUE '1'.
           05  PM-MARITAL-STAT         PIC X(01).
               88  MARRIED                         VALUE 'M'.
               88  SINGLE                          VALUE 'S'.
           05  PM-NBR-DEPEND           PIC 9(02).
           05  PM-PAY-RATE             PIC 9(06)V99.
           05  PM-HOUR-WORK            PIC 9(03)V99.
           05  PM-YTD-GROSS            PIC 9(06)V99.
           05  PM-YTD-DEDUCTS          PIC 9(05)V99.
           05                          PIC X(22).

       FD  EMPLOYEE-TRANSACTION
           RECORD CONTAINS 80 CHARACTERS
           DATA RECORD IS EMPL-TRANS-REC.
           COPY "C:\COBOL\EMPLTRAN.REC".
       01  EMPL-TRANS-REC.
           05  TRANS-CODE          PIC X(02).
               88  ADD-TRANS              VALUE 'A'.
               88  CHANGE-TRANS           VALUE 'C'.
               88  DELETE-TRANS           VALUE 'D'.
               88  CHANGE-NAME            VALUE 'CN'.
               88  CHANGE-DEPT-ID         VALUE 'CD'.
               88  CHANGE-STORE-ID        VALUE 'CS'.
               88  CHANGE-PAY-STATUS      VALUE 'CP'.
               88  CHANGE-MARITAL-STATUS  VALUE 'CM'.
               88  CHANGE-DEPEND-COUNT    VALUE 'CC'.
               88  CHANGE-PAY-RATE        VALUE 'CR'.
               88  CHANGE-HOUR-WORK       VALUE 'CH'.
               88  CHANGE-YTD-GROSS       VALUE 'CG'.
               88  CHANGE-YTD-DEDUCTS     VALUE 'CW'.
               88  VALID-TRANS            VALUE 'A' 'D' 'C'
                                                'CN' 'CD' 'CS'
                                                'CP' 'CM' 'CC'
                                                'CR' 'CH' 'CG'
                                                'CW'.
           05  ET-EMPLOYEE-NBR     PIC X(09).
           05  ET-EMPL-NAME.
               10  ET-LAST-NAME    PIC X(15).
               10  ET-FIRST-NAME   PIC X(15).
               10  ET-MIDDLE-INIT  PIC X(01).
           05  ADD-PAY-DATA.
               10  ADD-STORE-ID    PIC X(01).
               10  ADD-DEPT-ID     PIC X(03).
               10  ADD-PAY-STATUS  PIC X(01).
               10  ADD-MAR-STATUS  PIC X(01).
               10  ADD-NUM-DEPEND  PIC 9(02).
               10  ADD-PAY-RATE    PIC 9(06)V99.
               10  ADD-HOUR-WORK   PIC 9(03)V99.
           05                                   REDEFINES
ADD-PAY-DATA.
               10  CHG-DATA        PIC X(08).
               10  CHG-STORE-ID    PIC X(01)    REDEFINES CHG-DATA.
               10  CHG-DEPT-ID     PIC X(03)    REDEFINES CHG-DATA.

               10  CHG-PAY-STATUS  PIC X(01)    REDEFINES CHG-DATA.
               10  CHG-MAR-STATUS  PIC X(01)    REDEFINES CHG-DATA.
               10  CHG-NBR-DEPEND  PIC 9(02)    REDEFINES CHG-DATA.
               10  CHG-PAY-RATE    PIC 9(06)V99 REDEFINES CHG-DATA.
               10  CHG-HOUR-WORK   PIC 9(03)V99 REDEFINES CHG-DATA.
               10  CHG-YTD-GROSS   PIC 9(06)V99 REDEFINES CHG-DATA.
               10  CHG-YTD-DEDUCT  PIC 9(05)V99 REDEFINES CHG-DATA.
           05                      PIC X(17).
       FD  PRINT-FILE
           RECORD CONTAINS 80 CHARACTERS
           DATA RECORD IS PRINT-BUFFER.
       01  PRINT-BUFFER            PIC X(80).

       WORKING-STORAGE SECTION.

       01  CONDITION-SWITCHES.
           05  DATA-REC-SWITCH                 PIC X(01).
               88  DATA-REC-EOF                VALUE IS '1'.
           05  PREV-TRANS-REC                  PIC X(03).

       01  I-O-STATUS              PIC X(02)   VALUE SPACES.
           88  SUCCESSFUL-I-O                  VALUE IS '00'.
           88  READ-ERR-NOF                    VALUE IS '10'.
           88  ADD-ERR-DUPLICATE               VALUE IS '22'.

       000-UPDT-EMPL-FILE.
           INITIALIZE I-O-STATUS
           CALL "C:\COBOL\EMPLCOPY.INT".
           OPEN INPUT  EMPLOYEE-TRANSACTION
                I-O    EMPLOYEE-MASTER
                OUTPUT PRINT-FILE
           PERFORM 100-DO-INIT-TASKS
           PERFORM 200-UPDT-EMPL-REC
               UNTIL DATA-REC-EOF
           PERFORM 269-PRINT-ENDING-LINE
           CLOSE EMPLOYEE-TRANSACTION
                 EMPLOYEE-MASTER
                 PRINT-FILE
           STOP RUN
               .


       100-DO-INIT-TASKS.
           ACCEPT TODAYS-DATE FROM DATE YYYYMMDD
           MOVE TODAYS-DAY TO SHL-DATE-DAY
           MOVE TODAYS-MONTH TO SHL-DATE-MONTH
           MOVE TODAYS-YEAR TO SHL-DATE-YEAR
           PERFORM 260-PRINT-HEADER-LINES
           PERFORM 880-READ-TRANSACTION-RECORD.

       200-UPDT-EMPL-REC.
           EVALUATE TRUE
               WHEN ADD-TRANS
                   PERFORM 300-ADD-TRANS-REC
               WHEN DELETE-TRANS
                   PERFORM 301-DEL-TRANS-REC
               WHEN CHANGE-TRANS
                   PERFORM 302-CNG-TRANS-REC
           END-EVALUATE
           PERFORM 880-READ-TRANSACTION-RECORD
           .

       260-PRINT-HEADER-LINES.
           MOVE NBR-PAGES-PRINTED TO FHL-PAGE-COUNT
           MOVE FIRST-HEAD-LINE TO PRINT-BUFFER
           WRITE PRINT-BUFFER
               AFTER ADVANCING PAGE
           MOVE 1 TO SPACE-CONTROL
           MOVE SECOND-HEAD-LINE TO PRINT-BUFFER
           PERFORM 800-WRITE-PRINT-BUFFER
           MOVE 2 TO SPACE-CONTROL
           MOVE FIRST-COL-HEAD TO PRINT-BUFFER
           PERFORM 800-WRITE-PRINT-BUFFER
           MOVE 1 TO SPACE-CONTROL
           MOVE SECOND-COL-HEAD TO PRINT-BUFFER
           PERFORM 800-WRITE-PRINT-BUFFER
           MOVE 2 TO SPACE-CONTROL
           MOVE 6 TO NBR-LINES-PRINTED
           ADD 1 TO NBR-PAGES-PRINTED.

       261-PRINT-DETAIL-LINE.
           MOVE DETAIL-LINE TO PRINT-BUFFER
           PERFORM 800-WRITE-PRINT-BUFFER
           MOVE 1 TO SPACE-CONTROL
           .

       269-PRINT-ENDING-LINE.
           MOVE NORMAL-EOJ-MSG TO PRINT-BUFFER
           MOVE 2 TO SPACE-CONTROL
           PERFORM 800-WRITE-PRINT-BUFFER.


       300-ADD-TRANS-REC.
           INITIALIZE PAYROLL-MASTER
           PERFORM 881-CREATE-MASTER-RECORD
           WRITE PAYROLL-MASTER
           DISPLAY I-O-STATUS
           IF SUCCESSFUL-I-O
               PERFORM 353-PRINT-SUCCESSFUL-ADD
               WRITE PAYROLL-MASTER
           ELSE IF ADD-ERR-DUPLICATE
               PERFORM 354-PRINT-DUPLICATE-ADD
           END-IF

           .

       301-DEL-TRANS-REC.
           MOVE SPACES TO PM-EMPL-ID
           MOVE ET-EMPLOYEE-NBR TO PM-EMPL-ID
           READ EMPLOYEE-MASTER
           DISPLAY I-O-STATUS
           IF SUCCESSFUL-I-O
               PERFORM 350-PRINT-SUCCESSFUL-DELETE
               DELETE EMPLOYEE-MASTER RECORD
           ELSE
               PERFORM 352-PRINT-DEL-NOF
           END-IF
           .

       302-CNG-TRANS-REC.
           EVALUATE TRUE
               WHEN CHANGE-NAME
                   PERFORM 303-CHANGE-EMPLOYEE-NAME
               WHEN CHANGE-STORE-ID
                   PERFORM 304-CHANGE-STORE-ID
               WHEN CHANGE-DEPT-ID
                   PERFORM 305-CHANGE-DEPARTMENT-ID
               WHEN CHANGE-PAY-STATUS
                   PERFORM 306-CHANGE-PAY-STATUS
               WHEN CHANGE-MARITAL-STATUS
                   PERFORM 307-CHANGE-MAR-STATUS
               WHEN CHANGE-DEPEND-COUNT
                   PERFORM 308-CHANGE-NBR-DEPENDANTS
               WHEN CHANGE-PAY-RATE
                   PERFORM 309-CHANGE-PAY-RATE
               WHEN CHANGE-HOUR-WORK
                   PERFORM 310-CHANGE-HOUR-WORK
               WHEN CHANGE-YTD-GROSS
                   PERFORM 311-CHANGE-YTD-GROSS
               WHEN CHANGE-YTD-DEDUCTS
                   PERFORM 312-CHANGE-YTD-DEDUCTS
           END-EVALUATE
               .

       303-CHANGE-EMPLOYEE-NAME.
           MOVE ET-EMPLOYEE-NBR TO PM-EMPL-ID
           READ EMPLOYEE-MASTER
           IF SUCCESSFUL-I-O
               PERFORM 355-PRINT-NAME-CHANGE
               MOVE ET-EMPL-NAME TO PM-EMPL-NAME
               REWRITE PAYROLL-MASTER
           END-IF    .
       304-CHANGE-STORE-ID.
       .
       305-CHANGE-DEPARTMENT-ID.
       .
       306-CHANGE-PAY-STATUS.
       .
       307-CHANGE-MAR-STATUS.
       .
       308-CHANGE-NBR-DEPENDANTS.
       .
       309-CHANGE-PAY-RATE.
       .
       310-CHANGE-HOUR-WORK.
       .
       311-CHANGE-YTD-GROSS.
       .
       312-CHANGE-YTD-DEDUCTS.
       .

       350-PRINT-SUCCESSFUL-DELETE.
           MOVE PM-EMPL-ID TO DL-EMPL-ID
           MOVE REC-DEL TO DL-ACTION-MESSAGE
           MOVE TRANS-CODE TO DL-TRANS-CODE
           MOVE PM-EMPL-NAME TO DL-DATA-AREA
           PERFORM 261-PRINT-DETAIL-LINE
           MOVE SPACES TO DETAIL-LINE
           .

       351-PRINT-DEL-CORR-FAIL.
           MOVE PM-EMPL-ID TO DL-EMPL-ID
           MOVE DEL-FAIL-ERR1 TO DL-ACTION-MESSAGE
           MOVE TRANS-CODE TO DL-TRANS-CODE
           MOVE PM-EMPL-NAME TO DL-DATA-AREA
           PERFORM 261-PRINT-DETAIL-LINE
           MOVE SPACES TO DETAIL-LINE
           MOVE ET-EMPL-NAME TO DL-DATA-AREA
           MOVE DEL-FAIL-ERR2 TO DL-ACTION-MESSAGE
           PERFORM 261-PRINT-DETAIL-LINE
           MOVE SPACES TO DETAIL-LINE.

       352-PRINT-DEL-NOF.
           MOVE ET-EMPLOYEE-NBR TO DL-EMPL-ID
           MOVE ET-EMPL-NAME TO DL-DATA-AREA
           MOVE DEL-NOF-ERR TO DL-ACTION-MESSAGE
           MOVE TRANS-CODE TO DL-TRANS-CODE
           PERFORM 261-PRINT-DETAIL-LINE
           MOVE SPACES TO DETAIL-LINE.

       353-PRINT-SUCCESSFUL-ADD.
           MOVE PM-EMPL-ID TO DL-EMPL-ID
           MOVE PM-FIRST-NAME TO AND-FIRST-NAME
           MOVE PM-LAST-NAME TO AND-LAST-NAME
           MOVE PM-MIDDLE-INIT TO AND-MIDDLE-INIT
           MOVE ADD-NAME-DATAFIELD TO DL-DATA-AREA
           MOVE REC-ADD TO DL-ACTION-MESSAGE
           MOVE TRANS-CODE TO DL-TRANS-CODE
           PERFORM 261-PRINT-DETAIL-LINE
           MOVE SPACES TO DETAIL-LINE
           MOVE ADD-PAY-DATA TO DL-DATA-AREA
           PERFORM 261-PRINT-DETAIL-LINE
           MOVE SPACES TO DETAIL-LINE
           .

       354-PRINT-DUPLICATE-ADD.
           MOVE ET-EMPLOYEE-NBR TO PM-EMPL-ID
           READ EMPLOYEE-MASTER RECORD
           MOVE PM-EMPL-ID TO DL-EMPL-ID
           MOVE PM-LAST-NAME TO AND-LAST-NAME
           MOVE PM-FIRST-NAME TO AND-FIRST-NAME
           MOVE PM-MIDDLE-INIT TO AND-MIDDLE-INIT
           MOVE ADD-NAME-DATAFIELD TO DL-DATA-AREA
           MOVE TRANS-CODE TO DL-TRANS-CODE
           MOVE DUPL-ADD-ERR1 TO DL-ACTION-MESSAGE
           PERFORM 261-PRINT-DETAIL-LINE
           MOVE SPACES TO DETAIL-LINE
           MOVE ET-FIRST-NAME TO AND-FIRST-NAME
           MOVE ET-LAST-NAME TO AND-LAST-NAME
           MOVE ET-MIDDLE-INIT TO AND-MIDDLE-INIT
           MOVE ADD-NAME-DATAFIELD TO DL-DATA-AREA
           MOVE DUPL-ADD-ERR1 TO DL-ACTION-MESSAGE
           PERFORM 261-PRINT-DETAIL-LINE
           MOVE SPACES TO DETAIL-LINE
           .

       355-PRINT-NAME-CHANGE.
       MOVE PM-EMPL-ID TO DL-EMPL-ID
       MOVE TRANS-CODE TO DL-TRANS-CODE
       MOVE PM-FIRST-NAME TO CN1-FIRST-NAME
       MOVE PM-MIDDLE-INIT TO CN1-MIDDLE-INIT
       MOVE PM-LAST-NAME TO CN1-LAST-NAME
       MOVE CHANGE-NAME-1 TO DL-DATA-AREA
       MOVE MSG-NAME TO DL-ACTION-MESSAGE
       PERFORM 261-PRINT-DETAIL-LINE
       MOVE SPACES TO DETAIL-LINE
       MOVE ET-FIRST-NAME TO CN2-FIRST-NAME
       MOVE ET-MIDDLE-INIT TO CN2-MIDDLE-INIT
       MOVE ET-LAST-NAME TO CN2-LAST-NAME
       MOVE CHANGE-NAME-2 TO DL-DATA-AREA
       PERFORM 261-PRINT-DETAIL-LINE
       .

       800-WRITE-PRINT-BUFFER.
           WRITE PRINT-BUFFER
               AFTER ADVANCING SPACE-CONTROL LINES
               .

       880-READ-TRANSACTION-RECORD.
           READ EMPLOYEE-TRANSACTION
               AT END
                   MOVE '1' TO DATA-REC-SWITCH
                   .

       881-CREATE-MASTER-RECORD.
           MOVE ET-EMPLOYEE-NBR TO PM-EMPL-ID
           MOVE ET-EMPL-NAME TO PM-EMPL-NAME
           MOVE ADD-STORE-ID TO PM-STORE-ID
           MOVE ADD-DEPT-ID TO PM-DEPT-ID
           MOVE ADD-PAY-STATUS TO PM-PAY-STATUS
           MOVE ADD-MAR-STATUS TO PM-MARITAL-STAT
           MOVE ADD-NUM-DEPEND TO PM-NBR-DEPEND
           MOVE ADD-PAY-RATE TO PM-PAY-RATE
           MOVE ZERO TO PM-HOUR-WORK
           MOVE ZERO TO PM-YTD-GROSS
           MOVE ZERO TO PM-YTD-DEDUCTS
           .


Problems:  Doesn't seem to want to add a record, doesn't seem to want
to delete, doesn't seem to want to change a record.
```

## Thread: Page Eject to HP printer ?? — 6 this month / 7 total

_Topics: [`Help requests and how-to`](topics.md#help)_

### Page Eject to HP printer ??

- **From:** Bogy@Texas.NEt (Peter Gilchrist)
- **Date:** 1999-04-29T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3728a479.19716098@news.texas.net>`

```
What is the WS for printing a page eject contriol sequence in an
output document.

I beleive the HP control sequence is "esc"&l0H

so...

	05 FILLER    PIC 9  COMP-X  VALUE  027.
	05 FILLER    PIX X(4)              VALUE "&L0H".

but when this text file is sent to the hp printer, the page break does
not work.  I think its the "comp-x" ... but I'm not sure what
definition to use.
```

#### ↳ Re: Page Eject to HP printer ??

- **From:** pduboisREMOVETHIS@enteract.com (PAL3000)
- **Date:** 1999-04-29T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3728aa3a.9683929@news.enteract.com>`
- **References:** `<3728a479.19716098@news.texas.net>`

```
With microfocus, I setup WS hex codes like this

   01  PAGE-EJECT    PIC X(2)   X"FFFF".


Notice the X before the literal, and the size is in Bytes.

Hope this helps.

Paul


On Thu, 29 Apr 1999 18:31:02 GMT, Bogy@Texas.NEt (Peter Gilchrist)
wrote:

>What is the WS for printing a page eject contriol sequence in an
>output document.
…[10 more quoted lines elided]…
>definition to use.

--------------------------------------
To reply by email, please take out the
words REMOVETHIS from my email address
```

#### ↳ Re: Page Eject to HP printer ??

- **From:** "Warren Porter" <warrenp123@netdoor123.com>
- **Date:** 1999-04-29T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<hY2W2.1457$JC1.81838@axe.netdoor.com>`
- **References:** `<3728a479.19716098@news.texas.net>`

```

Peter Gilchrist wrote in message <3728a479.19716098@news.texas.net>...
>What is the WS for printing a page eject contriol sequence in an
>output document.
>
>I beleive the HP control sequence is "esc"&l0H


*****> 05 FILLER    PIC 9  COMP-X  VALUE  027. ******

You might not need that line at all.  Try:
   05 FILLER     PIC X(1)             VALUE X"1B".
> 05 FILLER    PIX X(4)              VALUE "&L0H".


Warren Porter -- Remove numbers to reply.
```

#### ↳ Re: Page Eject to HP printer ??

- **From:** "Donald Tees" <donald@willmack.com>
- **Date:** 1999-04-29T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<7gap79$ebm$1@news.igs.net>`
- **References:** `<3728a479.19716098@news.texas.net>`

```
A simple Ascii FormFeed will work fine.  However, if you want to
code up the escape sequences, I do it like so:
      *The HP setup strings
       01 PAGE-SETUP.
      *set page orientation Portrait/Landscape
           02 FILLER               PICTURE X VALUE X"1B".
           02 FILLER               PICTURE XX VALUE "&l".
           02 PAGE-ORIENTATION     PICTURE 9 VALUE 0.
           02 FILLER               PICTURE X VALUE "O".
      *set fixed font (non proportional)
           02 FILLER               PICTURE X VALUE X"1B".
           02 FILLER               PICTURE X(4) VALUE "(10U".
      *set to row 0
           02 FILLER               PICTURE X VALUE X"1B".
           02 FILLER               PICTURE X(4) VALUE "&a0R".
      *set to column 0
           02 FILLER               PICTURE X VALUE X"1B".
           02 FILLER               PICTURE X(4) VALUE "&a0C".
           02 FILLER               PICTURE X VALUE "O".
      *set number of copies
           02 FILLER               PICTURE X VALUE X"1B".
           02 FILLER               PICTURE XX VALUE "&l".
           02 NUMBER-OF-COPIES     PICTURE 99 VALUE 1.
           02 FILLER               PICTURE X VALUE "X".
      *set character size
           02 FILLER               PICTURE X VALUE X"1B".
           02 FILLER               PICTURE X(4) VALUE "(s0p".
           02 CHARS-PER-INCH       PICTURE 99.99 VALUE "16.67".
           02 FILLER               PICTURE X(11) VALUE "h8.5v0s0b0T".
      *lines per inch
           02 FILLER               PICTURE X VALUE X"1B".
           02 FILLER               PICTURE XX VALUE "&l".
           02 LINES-PER-INCH       PICTURE 99.99 VALUE "08.80".
           02 FILLER               PICTURE X VALUE "D".
       01 PAGE-SETUP-ARRAY REDEFINES PAGE-SETUP.
           02 PAGE-BYTE            PICTURE X OCCURS 57 TIMES.

Peter Gilchrist wrote in message <3728a479.19716098@news.texas.net>...
>What is the WS for printing a page eject contriol sequence in an
>output document.
…[10 more quoted lines elided]…
>definition to use.
```

<a id="t-3b9fa7795c"></a>
## Thread: OLE Data Types of Microfocus COBOL — 2 msgs

_Topics: [`Compilers and vendors`](topics.md#compilers)_

### OLE Data Types of Microfocus COBOL

- **From:** John <jtimber@hotmail.com>
- **Date:** 1999-04-29T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3728F5E2.9E4B1BE6@hotmail.com>`

```
Hi ,

I am seeking help on passing data to a COBOL object from a VB
application.

What is the OLE data type I should use to pass a copy member to the
cobol
COM module?  In my VB calling program, the data is user-defined
structure.

Thank for any input.

Keith
```

#### ↳ Re: OLE Data Types of Microfocus COBOL

- **From:** Stephen.Biggs@merant.com (Steve Biggs)
- **Date:** 1999-04-30T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<7gbv0h$mat@hyperion.mfltd.co.uk>`
- **References:** `<3728F5E2.9E4B1BE6@hotmail.com>`

```
jtimber@hotmail.com wrote:
>I am seeking help on passing data to a COBOL object from a VB
>application.
…[4 more quoted lines elided]…
>structure.

Hi Keith,
Firstly, OLE doesn't handle structures very well (the standard COM 
marshaller would need to know the structure of the data in order to do 
things like AddRef interfaces etc.) and is far better with basic data 
types.

I don't think VB 5 allows passing user-defined data structures (well, I 
couldn't get it to work, but then the errors VB gives completely baffle 
me at the best of times!). 

VB 6 uses the new COM VT_RECORD type (which were introduced in NT 4.0 SP4, 
IIRC, and might not be supported over DCOM). We don't yet support this 
easily in Object COBOL but it is possible to access the data with work.[1]

I'd recommend either passing the data items as separate parameters.
Apart from anything else, this gives COM less work to do, so should give 
better performance. Plus you only have to pass the data members that 
actually matter to the method.

You could also consider creating SafeArrays (VB arrays) of types (maybe 
even Variant types) - these will be received on the COBOL side as 
"olesafea" objects, with a set of methods for accessing the data.

[1] If you *really* want to know how it might be done (and it involves 
some deep COM/API programming), send me an e-mail. I'm assuming Net 
Express 2.0 or above, here.

Steve.
```

---

## Thread: Micro Focus Sequential File Maintence Example Needed — 3 msgs

### Micro Focus Sequential File Maintence Example Needed

- **From:** memoir@megsinet.net

```
program.  Im using MF pc cobol.  Examples in the book arent helping.

Name, Phone, Debit/Credit, Balance.    Not urgent, but could use the

memoir@megsinet.net
```

#### ↳ Re: Micro Focus Sequential File Maintence Example Needed

- **From:** redsky@ibm.net (Thane Hubbell)
- **Date:** 1999-04-29T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3728e238.72156220@news1.ibm.net>`
- **References:** `<372877fc.213554357@news.megsinet.net>`

```
On Thu, 29 Apr 1999 15:17:59 GMT, memoir@megsinet.net wrote:

>Does anyone have a good example of a Sequential File Maintenance
>program.  Im using MF pc cobol.  Examples in the book arent helping.
>Need an  Add, Change, Delete program example.  Doing one with Acct#,
>Name, Phone, Debit/Credit, Balance.    Not urgent, but could use the
>help.

Run (Don't walk) to your nearest bookstore or library and look at Hour
18 of Sams Teach Yourself COBOL in 24 hours.  The entire lesson is
devoted to master file updating explaining, with multiple examples,
exactly what you are asking about!

When you look at the book (if you are in a book store and not a
library!) buy it!
```

```
On Fri, 30 Apr 1999 13:29:22, Ken Foskey <waratah@zip.com.au> wrote:

> Thane Hubbell wrote:
> > Cindy!  Glad to see you took my advice and corrected the name, MQ series
> 
> 
> What is MQ series???

MQ series is a Message Queueing system developed and sold by IBM that 
allows program to program communication across platforms.  (Mainframe,
VMS, NT, UNIX, OS/2 etc etc are all supported).

-------------------------
Trust the computer industry to shorten "Year 2000" to Y2K.  It was 
this
kind of thinking that caused the problem in the first place.

Visit my updated website at
http://www.geocities.com/Eureka/2006/
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Seattle - MQ Serials Specialist need urgently - up to $70/hr

- **From:** swiegand@neo.rr.com (SkippyPB)
- **Date:** 1999-04-30T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** ab.jobs,bc.jobs,can.jobs,comp.lang.cobol,comp.lang.java.corba
- **Message-ID:** `<2C5343A33636CAA1.403BA015F3B19627.B84C70524C58340A@library-proxy.airnews.net>`
- **References:** `<Va4W2.7431$e4.1558@cabot.ops.attcanada.net> <3728e2ea.72334668@news1.ibm.net> <3729B032.F42E55B3@zip.com.au>`

```
On Fri, 30 Apr 1999 23:29:22 +1000, Ken Foskey <waratah@zip.com.au>
enlightened us:

>Thane Hubbell wrote:
>> Cindy!  Glad to see you took my advice and corrected the name, MQ series
>
>
>What is MQ series???

MQ series is an IBM product that allows communication from one
platform to another.  MQM MVS/ESA runs as a subsytem under MVS/ESA.
It is available to applications running under CICS/ESA, MVS/TSO, MVS
batch and VSE.  It is especially useful in a client server/mainframe
scenario.

I just started working on a project a couple of months ago utilizing
MQ messaging.  It's pretty cool stuff.

Regards,

          ////
         (o o)
-oOO--(_)--OOo-

For Sale: Parachute. Only used once, never opened, small stain.

 Steve
```

<a id="t-fb89be03d4"></a>
## Thread: MF COBOL v AcuCOBOL etc — 3 msgs

_Topics: [`Compilers and vendors`](topics.md#compilers)_

### MF COBOL v AcuCOBOL etc

- **From:** "Rick Price" <rick@hpdi.demon.co.uk>
- **Date:** 1999-04-29T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<925408676.7227.0.nnrp-13.9e98b131@news.demon.co.uk>`

```
We currently have an appilication that was originally based on
WANG VS, then moved to AS400 and then to unix.

I.e. it works on multiple platforms.

We handle screens ourselves.  We have also isolated IO.

We went for MF COBOL (sometime ago) on unix because this seemed to be the
standard on the unix platforms (hp and aix) that we wanted to support..

I must admit that MFCOBOL ISAM matched the way we processed files.

However, lately I've been worried about the way MF COBOL is going.

I've looked at the acucobol site but it doesn't give any information on the
way ISAM files are handled.   I don't know about other unix enabled COBOL,

These queries are unix specific.  Does anybody have information on:

Cost (runtime) of MFCOBOL v other COBOLS.

Views on ISAM files especially how they handle duplicate alternate keys.

Views on ISAM files especiallly how they handle optional alternate keys eg
if a = x then do not include on the key path.

How they handle files > 2 GB.

We have a problem in so far as we have an application that works but I feel
we are reaching some kind of limit in terms of what MFCOBOL can do or what
they are willing to do on unix.

I get a strong feeling that they are windows oriented and unix processing is
accidental.

Regards
Rick
```

#### ↳ Re: MF COBOL v AcuCOBOL etc

- **From:** "Cheesle" <cheesle@online.NoSpamPlease.no>
- **Date:** 1999-04-29T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<7gab8u$4u6$1@news.cerf.net>`
- **References:** `<925408676.7227.0.nnrp-13.9e98b131@news.demon.co.uk>`

```
Rick Price wrote in message
<925408676.7227.0.nnrp-13.9e98b131@news.demon.co.uk>...
>We currently have an appilication that was originally based on
>WANG VS, then moved to AS400 and then to unix.

I know a bank in Norway that successfully move from Wang to Acucobol, you
should find the story about them on www.acucorp.com, otherwise I know that a
former manager of their development team frequently visits this newsgroup,
he may be able to tell you something if he sees this mail. The bank's name
is BN Bank.

>I've looked at the acucobol site but it doesn't give any information on the
>way ISAM files are handled.

>Cost (runtime) of MFCOBOL v other COBOLS.

That you would probably need to question Acucorp about, I have no idea.

>Views on ISAM files especially how they handle duplicate alternate keys.
>Views on ISAM files especiallly how they handle optional alternate keys eg

I am not certain if I understand your question, duplicates are allowed if
specified.

>How they handle files > 2 GB.

They do support files bigger than 2GB.

>We have a problem in so far as we have an application that works but I feel
>we are reaching some kind of limit in terms of what MFCOBOL can do or what
>they are willing to do on unix.
>I get a strong feeling that they are windows oriented and unix processing
is
>accidental.


Well, I don't know anything about MF Cobol, I do however know that Acucobols
commitment to Unix is strong, in fact so strong that I have complained about
it (I am a PC programmer) :-)

Cheesle
```

#### ↳ Re: MF COBOL v AcuCOBOL etc

- **From:** "Erlend Moen" <erlend@disys.no>
- **Date:** 1999-04-30T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<7gbdm0$rtp$1@troll.powertech.no>`
- **References:** `<925408676.7227.0.nnrp-13.9e98b131@news.demon.co.uk>`

```
Rick Price wrote in the message
<925408676.7227.0.nnrp-13.9e98b131@news.demon.co.uk>...
>We currently have an appilication that was originally based on
>WANG VS, then moved to AS400 and then to unix.
>
>I.e. it works on multiple platforms.

Well - let me tell you about my experience in converting/adapting to Unix.

I used to work in a bank in Norway where we had Wang VS-Cobol-applications
running. These applications was self-developed, so we had access to all
source.
As we grew larger (the bank, not the emloyees!) the Wang-system was
overloaded and we decided to switch platform. We had several candidates, but
ended up with Acucobol running on SCO-Unix on Compaq-servers. We even went
to Munich in Germany to test the estimated load on our applications on
Compaqs test-lab, a test that convinced us that we had taken the right
decision.

I was "interviewed" by Acucorp after we finally had ported our application
telling about this (http://www.acucobol.com/Partners/Success/BNBank.html).
Although it may look like an ad for Acucorp, I stand for most of the text.
For what it's worth, I can recommend AcuCorp for two reasons: They have a
wonderful compiler with lots and lots of useful possibilities and they have
excellent service if you reach into trouble of any kind.

Don't hesitate to ask me any more if you need further information!

Erlend
```

<a id="t-11bf852c65"></a>
## Thread: Improve your career opportunities with FREE Online COBOL Certifications — 1 msgs

_Topics: [`Jobs, careers, recruiting, salary`](topics.md#jobs)_

### Improve your career opportunities with FREE Online COBOL Certifications

- **From:** Ludlow@NewsReader.Com (Ann Marie Ludlow)
- **Date:** 1999-04-29T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<19990428232319.750$kc@newsreader.com>`

```
Dear COBOL Programmer,

Improve your career opportunities through TekMetrics free technical
certifications!

Through April, TekMetrics has extended its free service of COBOL Programmer
certifications for OS/390 or UDB conducted through its internet-based CAT
engine.  Simply go to http://www.tekmetrics.com/cert/certtop.html to order
your free certification for either test.  You can either elect to take the
test right away, or come back later, using the PIN you are provided when
you order.

To qualify as a Certified COBOL Programmer, you must pass the examination
with a score of 2.75 or higher.  To be certified as a Master COBOL
Programmer, you must obtain a score of 4.00 or higher.  At the end of the
test, your score will be provided (with a scoring range of 1-5).  Once
certified, you can add the logo associated with the test (standard or
master logo, depending on how well you do) to your resume or website.
Furthermore, those that pass prior to 4/19 will be mailed a certificate,
once again, at no cost (after which time their may be a nominal fee for the
shipping of the certificates). There's no catch.  We're simply trying to
prove a concept during a beta trial period.  Thus far, the feedback has
been extremely positive!

Why bother getting certified by TekMetrics?

1)  It's free and it's fun.  They won't be free forever, but they are now.
So take advantage of them.  Furthermore, those who have completed the tests
generally indicate that they enjoyed the experience and considered both the
delivery of the test and the items themselves to be of high quality.

2)  Over 4000 technical workers from around the world are ordering our
certifications each week, and that number is steadily rising.

3)  Find out both your strengths and weaknesses in COBOL.  At the end of
the test, you can request specifics on how you did, by subtopic.  And yes,
that too is free.

4) Strut your stuff.  See how you compare to our Top Ten List!

All we ask for in return is your feedback at the end of the exam.  We will
be continually focusing on the improvement of the delivery of the test and
quality of the questions.  Any feedback you can provide, positive or
negative, will help us greatly.

Another FAQ is, "How do you know the person who supposedly took the test
was actually the same person?  What if it was his hotshot COBOL Programmer
brother, sister or friend?  In other words, what is to prevent him from
cheating?"  Our solution:  we allow employers to spotcheck the technical
workers with a short test at no cost at their corporate locations, if they
so choose.  Corporations have unanimously applauded this solution.

Lastly, data you provide in the certification process is not used without
your permission other than to store your certification record and verify to
valid employers at their request.  We do, however, publish names on our
site in a Top Ten Scores list for each category, but no addresses, phone
numbers, email, etc. are shown.  For more information, see our privacy
statement at http://www.tekmetrics.com/cert/privacy.html

Well, that's pretty much it!  We're trying to establish a new
cost-effective and convenient alternative to today's certification
environment.  During this beta trial period, you will be the benefactor of
this fun and challenging experience.

If you have any questions/concerns regarding COBOL certification, please
contact Deborah Manley at <mailto:deborah.manley@tekmetrics.com>.

Good luck and thank you.

Ann Marie Ludlow
TekMetrics, Inc.
www.tekmetrics.com <http://www.tekmetrics.com>
```

## Thread: Search statement — 5 msgs

### Search statement

- **From:** petsto@my-dejanews.com
- **Date:** 1999-04-30T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<7gbp6i$7qm$1@nnrp1.dejanews.com>`

```
Hello all,

I have a problem with a SEARCH statement.
I have a 2-dimensional table, and I need to find an element in the main table,
then one in the sub-table, the one according to the element found in the main
table.

For example I find element 5 of the main table is the one I need. I now have
to search in element 5 the sub table for a correct element.

My question : In the second search statement, do I have to include the index ?
(I enclosed the source :-))
For further information : I am using Cobol compiler HP COBOL II/XL for MPE/iX
on HP3000.
Please reply to Peter.Stoops@AG.BE



The source :
039000 01 grpdep-table.
039100     02 grpdep-elem       OCCURS 99 INDEXED by grpdep-index.
039200      03 grpdep-numimm    PIC X(4).
039300      03 mildepspe-numimm PIC S9(9) COMP.
039400      03 numniv-elem    OCCURS 171 INDEXED by numniv-index.
039500        05 numniv-niveau  PIC X(4).
039600        05 codx4-niveau  PIC X(4).
039700        05 typuni-niveau  PIC X(2).
039800        05 surloctot-niveau  PIC S9(9) COMP.
039900        05 mildepspe-niveau  PIC S9(9) COMP.
040000        05 surloctotbrut-niveau  PIC S9(9) COMP.


151500               SET grpdep-index TO 1
151600*              DISPLAY grpdep-numimm(grpdep-index)
151700               SEARCH grpdep-elem VARYING grpdep-index
151800                   AT END
151900                      MOVE "grpdep-table-surloc" TO ed-field-name
152000                      MOVE 4 TO error-number
152100                      PERFORM error-write THRU error-write-exit
152200                   WHEN grpdep-numimm(grpdep-index) =
152300                        grpdepspe-sur(grpdep-index-surloc)
152400*                       DISPLAY "==== ",
152500*                           grpdep-numimm(1)
152600                         SET numniv-index TO 1
152700                         SEARCH numniv-elem VARYING numniv-index
OR ?????
152700                         SEARCH numniv-elem (grpdep-index)
                                                  VARYING numniv-index
152800                     AT END
152900*                     DISPLAY numniv-sur
153000*                     DISPLAY numniv-niveau(1 1)
153100*                     DISPLAY numniv-niveau(1 30)
153200*                    SET display-index TO grpdep-index
153300*                    DISPLAY "hihi ", display-index
153400                      MOVE "grpdep-table-surlo2" TO ed-field-name
153500                      MOVE 4 TO error-number
153600                      PERFORM error-write THRU error-write-exit
153700
..............
157000                         END-SEARCH
157100               END-SEARCH
Thanks in advance for your help

-----------== Posted via Deja News, The Discussion Network ==----------
http://www.dejanews.com/       Search, Read, Discuss, or Start Your Own
```

#### ↳ Re: Search statement

- **From:** Ed Stevens <Ed.Stevens@nmm.nissan-usa.com>
- **Date:** 1999-04-30T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol

```
>
…[60 more quoted lines elided]…
>
```

#### ↳ Re: Search statement

- **From:** "V. Moss" <vmoss@affirm.co.uk>
- **Date:** 1999-04-30T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<925476177.19952.0.nnrp-08.9e987e6e@news.demon.co.uk>`
- **References:** `<7gbp6i$7qm$1@nnrp1.dejanews.com>`

```
I think you do need to refer to the index in your second search statement.
When you want to refer to an element in the subtable you will need to use
both indexes as in(grpdep-index, numniv index).

I can't see a 'When' on your second search, is it after the perform?

HTH

Vanessa
```

#### ↳ Re: Search statement

- **From:** redsky@ibm.net (Thane Hubbell)
- **Date:** 1999-04-30T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3729e27a.47262279@news1.ibm.net>`
- **References:** `<7gbp6i$7qm$1@nnrp1.dejanews.com>`

```
On Fri, 30 Apr 1999 08:21:41 GMT, petsto@my-dejanews.com wrote:

>Hello all,
>
…[14 more quoted lines elided]…
>

You really don't need the varying on either table.

The search of the second level table will be based on the current
value of the index of the first table.  Your search line is correct.
I didn't see a WHEN for the second search, however.  For the WHEN you
must fully qualify the element being tested (all index levels).

You can see an example of this in my book, Sams Teach Yourself COBOL
in 24 Hours, Chapter 12.  If I can help you further, let me know!
```

---

<a id="t-907b5af155"></a>
## Thread: Call EXE files in MF — 2 this month / 4 total

### Call EXE files in MF

- **From:** "Euromercante" <EUROMERCANTE@MAIL.TELEPAC.PT>
- **Date:** 1999-04-30T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<7gc4dn$73n$1@duke.telepac.pt>`

```
How can call a program  ".EXE"  in NetEXpress V2.

Thanks
```

#### ↳ Re: Call EXE files in MF

- **From:** "Gael Wilson" <Gael.Wilson@merant.com>
- **Date:** 1999-04-30T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<7gc5ne$om9@hyperion.mfltd.co.uk>`
- **References:** `<7gc4dn$73n$1@duke.telepac.pt>`

```
You cannot call an EXE, as an EXE is the main module of an application. If
you want to call a program it has to be a DLL. However, if you want to start
another application you can execute the .EXE by using the run-time call
x"91" function 35 or CBL_EXEC_RUN_UNIT .

I hope this helps.


Euromercante wrote in message <7gc4dn$73n$1@duke.telepac.pt>...
>How can call a program  ".EXE"  in NetEXpress V2.
>
>Thanks
>
>
```

<a id="t-56311692f2"></a>
## Thread: MicroFocus App Printer Problem — 3 msgs

_Topics: [`Compilers and vendors`](topics.md#compilers)_

### MicroFocus App Printer Problem

- **From:** "Michael Lucich" <mal@internetx.net>
- **Date:** 1999-04-30T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<MIjW2.831$3h1.5984@newsfeed.slurp.net>`

```
Our users are having a problem printing in reports in a landscape format
from one of our applications which has been ported to MicroFocus COBOL.
This problem only occurs on certain printers.  It's been observed that the
printers associated with this problem are those which are Postscript
compatible, but I can't say for certain that that�s true.  This problem
happens with a Xerox Doc Center 220/230 PS2 printer.  It also happens with
some HP printers, e.g. with an HP LaserJet 4Plus.  The program works fine
with many other printers, and serves to allow the application user to print
reports from the application.

We are not sure when the problem started.  The initial version of the
application was a 16-bit version fielded at a few of our sites, and seemed
to work fine.  Then the 32-bit version was developed and fielded at a few
more sites and seemed to work fine.  As it was fielded at more and more
sites, we began to receive reports about
this problem.

The error is:
"Execution error:  file 'PRTTEST.gnt'
 error code: 114, pc=0, call=24, seg=0
 114  Attempt to access item beyond bounds of memory (signal 11)"
Press Enter to continue.  After pressing enter, the program aborts.

The program in question uses calls to: "CBL_OPEN_FILE",
"PC_WIN_OPEN_PRINTER_EXT", "PC_WIN_SET_PFONT", "PC_WIN_WRITE_PRINTER", and
so on.

The program is compiled with MicroFocus COBOL:
Workbench v 4.0.32
Advanced Organizer Initial Release, version DETV 1.1.25
Animator V2 ANM 8.b.cc
Dialog 2.5.64

We did not develop this system, but do have access to the source code.  The
developer is overloaded with work and so now we have begun to look into
this.

Any ideas or suggestions will be appreciated!

Mike
```

#### ↳ Re: MicroFocus App Printer Problem

- **From:** "Donald Tees" <donald@willmack.com>
- **Date:** 1999-04-30T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<7gcn6c$l5n$1@news.igs.net>`
- **References:** `<MIjW2.831$3h1.5984@newsfeed.slurp.net>`

```
I just discovered an interesting windows bug that might be the culprit. It
seems that windows will handle file names like "LPT1:" only when *all*
file names in the run unit adhere to 8.3 style names, EVEN FOR DIRECTORIES.

Example.  Go into a dos window, and copy a test file to a captured printer
port.  IE:  Copy abc lpt1:

This will work from a directory called "temp", but not from a directory
called "temp.extension".  As soon as the file being copied does not
adhere to the 8.3 rule throughout (even though it is not mentioned in
the command line), then the device "LPT1" no longer seems to get
recognized.

I just had a print routine fail, and it failed because I was running the
program from an area with an extended name.  As soon as I executed
it from an area with a DOS compatible name, the problem goes away.

Bloody silly, but then who the hell knows what that damned OS is doing?

Michael Lucich wrote in message ...
>Our users are having a problem printing in reports in a landscape format
>from one of our applications which has been ported to MicroFocus COBOL.
…[40 more quoted lines elided]…
>
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: MicroFocus App Printer Problem

- **From:** "Michael Lucich" <mal@internetx.net>
- **Date:** 1999-04-30T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<kZmW2.1028$3h1.5141@newsfeed.slurp.net>`
- **References:** `<MIjW2.831$3h1.5984@newsfeed.slurp.net> <7gcn6c$l5n$1@news.igs.net>`

```
When the program has this problem printing a certain file on a certain
printer, it blows up.  I should have mentioned that the user can get back
into the application and print the same report file on another printer
without a problem.  The problem only occurs with certain printers, and only
when printing reports in landscape mode.  The same report can also be
printed in portrait mode on any printer without a problem.  So the file name
or the name of the directory the file is in do not seem to be the trigger.
Thanks.  Sorry I omitted those facts.

Donald Tees wrote in message <7gcn6c$l5n$1@news.igs.net>...
>I just discovered an interesting windows bug that might be the culprit. It
>seems that windows will handle file names like "LPT1:" only when *all*
>file names in the run unit adhere to 8.3 style names, EVEN FOR DIRECTORIES.
>
etc.
```
