#### ↳ Re: Fujitsu & Call Win32API

- **From:** redsky@ibm.net (Thane Hubbell)
- **Date:** 1999-04-30T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3729e365.47497323@news1.ibm.net>`
- **References:** `<7gcjd4$i9r$1@duke.telepac.pt>`

```
On Fri, 30 Apr 1999 16:53:07 +0100, "Euromercante"
<EUROMERCANTE@MAIL.TELEPAC.PT> wrote:

>How can call win32api in Fujitsu Cobol?
>
>For example maximize windows and minimize .
>
>Thanks

This *appears* (I'm still waiting for resolution from Fujitsu support)
to be something you CANNOT do.  You can call the API from 7 ways to
sunday, but you can't "RegisterClass" and "CreateWindow".  I suppose
if you could get the handle of the window you were in you could make
the proper API call to maximize and minimize, but actually CREATING a
Window appears to be an impossibility - why?  No way to get a pointer
to the entry point of the program to process the messages for the
window.  I have submitted this to Fujitsu support and they are
investigating.  PowerCOBOL does it somehow, but I don't know how.
```

<a id="t-04e82956d5"></a>
## Thread: EOF error — 4 msgs

### EOF error

- **From:** dupavoy@aol.combatSPAM (Dupavoy)
- **Date:** 1999-04-30T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<19990430122433.18640.00000437@ng07.aol.com>`

```
I get the following error when the program runs:
Run time error number: 018

It has something to do with the program running 
out of data to read.

Here is the program:
         
        Input-Output section.

        file-control.

           SELECT PB3M-FILE-IN  ASSIGN to "pb3m.dat".
           SELECT PRG2-FILE-OUT ASSIGN to "prg2-out.dat".
           ORGANIZATION is INDEXED
           ACCESS MODE  is SEQUENTIAL
           RECORD KEY   is NewBookCode.
      

       DATA DIVISION.

       FILE SECTION.

       FD PB3M-FILE-IN.
       01 pb3m-rec-in.
          05 BookCode         PIC X(6).
          05 BookTitle        PIC X(16).
          05 BookAuthor       PIC X(9).
          05 BookListPrice    PIC 99v99.
          05 BookCopiesOnHand PIC 9(2).
          05 BookLastActivity PIC 9(6) comp-3.

       FD PRG2-FILE-OUT.
       01 prg2-rec-out.
          05 NewBookCode         PIC X(6).
          05 NewBookTitle        PIC X(16).
          05 NewBookAuthor       PIC X(9).
          05 NewBookListPrice    PIC 99v99.
          05 NewBookCopiesOnHand PIC 9(2).
          05 NewBookLastActivity PIC 9(6) comp-3.


       WORKING-STORAGE SECTION.

       01 EOF PIC X(3)  value 'NO '.

 
       PROCEDURE DIVISION.

       MAIN-MODULE.
           PERFORM INITIALIZATION.
           PERFORM CREATE
                   UNTIL EOF = 'YES'.
           PERFORM END-OF-JOB.
           STOP RUN.


       CREATE.
           READ PB3M-FILE-IN
                AT END MOVE 'YES' TO EOF.

           MOVE pb3m-rec-in TO prg2-rec-out.

           WRITE prg2-rec-out
                INVALID KEY DISPLAY 'INVALID RECORD: ', pb3m-rec-in.


       INITIALIZATION.
           OPEN INPUT  PB3M-FILE-IN
                OUTPUT PRG2-FILE-OUT.


       END-OF-JOB.
           CLOSE PB3M-FILE-IN
                         PRG2-FILE-OUT.


As you can see I am using EOF to end the program.
Am I doing something wrong?


***********************************
-CPR--subscribe@onelist.com   
 - Computer PRogramming list

PCHelpDesk-subscribe@onelist.com 
- PC software/hardware list
*******************************************
```

#### ↳ Re: EOF error

- **From:** Pete Wirfs <petwir@saif.com>
- **Date:** 1999-04-30T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3729E046.463E@saif.com>`
- **References:** `<19990430122433.18640.00000437@ng07.aol.com>`

```
Right after your read statement, you move your data out of your FD
section to another location.  This instruction is illegal when the file
is currently at EOF.

Try using the "READ ... INTO ..." form of the READ instruction.

Pete
```

#### ↳ Re: EOF error

- **From:** "Alan Russell" <RUSSELAH@apci.com>
- **Date:** 1999-04-30T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<7gcnei$ho6@netnews1.apci.com>`
- **References:** `<19990430122433.18640.00000437@ng07.aol.com>`

```
This is exactly why the "exit in the middle" without a priming read gets
people into problems.  When the program gets to end of file the record-area
for the input file is no longer defined.  The program must then "skip" to
move and write statements.  A better structure is:

>           PERFORM INITIALIZATION.
             READ PB3M-FILE-IN
                   AT END MOVE 'YES' TO EOF.
>           PERFORM CREATE
>                   UNTIL EOF = 'YES'.
…[12 more quoted lines elided]…
>


Dupavoy wrote in message <19990430122433.18640.00000437@ng07.aol.com>...
>I get the following error when the program runs:
>Run time error number: 018
…[86 more quoted lines elided]…
>
```

#### ↳ Re: EOF error

- **From:** "William M. Klein" <wmklein@inospam.netcom.com>
- **Date:** 1999-04-30T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<7gd81u$k53@dfw-ixnews8.ix.netcom.com>`
- **References:** `<19990430122433.18640.00000437@ng07.aol.com>`

```
Lots of others have replied talking about the problem - and giving
solutions.  My approach (assuming you have an 85 Standard compiler) would be
to change your create paragraph to:

           READ PB3M-FILE-IN
                AT END
                      MOVE 'YES' TO EOF
               Not at End
                     MOVE pb3m-rec-in TO prg2-rec-out
                     WRITE prg2-rec-out
                           INVALID KEY
                                DISPLAY 'INVALID RECORD: ', pb3m-rec-in
                     End-Write
           End-Read
             .
```

---

## Thread: Blanking out 2nd line of Ctrl Break — 4 this month / 5 total

### Blanking out 2nd line of Ctrl Break

- **From:** dginc@tds.net
- **Date:** 1999-04-30T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<7gcln3$cnp@news2.tds.net>`

```
how do you blank out say the person name you have a single ctrl break on.  
like i want to print my report and ctrl break after each name, but i want it 
to print the name only on the first line any time after the first line if 
there is more records for that person leave only the name field blank, once 
new name is started same with it, print once and blank name on any record 
after it.
thanks
```

#### ↳ Re: Blanking out 2nd line of Ctrl Break

- **From:** Pete Wirfs <petwir@saif.com>
- **Date:** 1999-04-30T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3729DF76.704D@saif.com>`
- **References:** `<7gcln3$cnp@news2.tds.net>`

```
dginc@tds.net wrote:
> 
> how do you blank out say the person name you have a single ctrl break on.
…[5 more quoted lines elided]…
> thanks

Define a new variable (PREVIOUS-NAME) in working storage.

RECORD-READ-PROCESSING:
     PREVIOUS-NAME = RECORD-NAME
     READ NEXT RECORD

PRINT-PROCESSING:
     IF PREVIOUS-NAME NOT = RECORD-NAME
        move RECORD-NAME to name print area
     ELSE
        move spaces      to name print area
     END-IF
     print the detail line


You could also modify this a bit so it would also print the
name if you are printing the first detail line of a new page.

Pete
```

#### ↳ Re: Blanking out 2nd line of Ctrl Break

- **From:** Howard Brazee <brazee@NOSPAMhome.com>
- **Date:** 1999-04-30T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3729E6DA.B6ADE1F0@NOSPAMhome.com>`
- **References:** `<7gcln3$cnp@news2.tds.net>`

```
dginc@tds.net wrote:
> 
> how do you blank out say the person name you have a single ctrl break on.
…[5 more quoted lines elided]…
> thanks

Try something like this:

  read file
  move 'garbage' to hold-name                      
  perform print-routine until file-eof
...
print-routine.
  if file-name = hold-name
     move space   to print-name
  else
     perform print-new-name
     move file-name   to print-name
                         hold-name
  end-if
  perform print detail-line
```

#### ↳ Re: Blanking out 2nd line of Ctrl Break

- **From:** "Donald Tees" <donald@willmack.com>
- **Date:** 1999-04-30T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<7gcvvk$oa6$1@news.igs.net>`
- **References:** `<7gcln3$cnp@news2.tds.net>`

```
You would do something like the following:

      perform report-customer-transaction-set until
          there-are-no-more-transactions.

  report-customer-transaction-set.
      perform report-customer-name.
      perform report-customer-transaction.

  report-customer-name.
      if customer-name is equal to last-customer-name
          continue
      else
          perform do-actual-printing
          move customer-name to last-customer-name.

  report-customer-transaction.
       perform do-actual-printing-two.
       perform get-next-transaction.

dginc@tds.net wrote in message <7gcln3$cnp@news2.tds.net>...
>how do you blank out say the person name you have a single ctrl break on.
>like i want to print my report and ctrl break after each name, but i want
it
>to print the name only on the first line any time after the first line if
>there is more records for that person leave only the name field blank, once
>new name is started same with it, print once and blank name on any record
>after it.
>thanks
```

<a id="t-0deed0ac71"></a>
## Thread: copies to printer (Fujitsu) — 1 msgs

_Topics: [`Compilers and vendors`](topics.md#compilers)_

### copies to printer (Fujitsu)

- **From:** "Donald Tees" <donald@willmack.com>
- **Date:** 1999-04-30T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<7gcp4p$m30$1@news.igs.net>`

```
Further to that last, re the copy routines.  The bug gets sillier and
sillier.  It appears that it is how you change to the directory that affects
whether the device "LPT" gets recognized.  The error exists in both
DOS (command.com) and in the Fujitsu copy routine. here is an example.
The following was grabbed by a screen capture program, and is my
exact test as done.

Q:\>cd areaname.test
Q:\areaname.test>copy test.rpt lpt1:
Path not found - lpt1
        0 file(s) copied
Q:\areaname.test>cd\areana~1.tes
Q:\areaname.test>copy test.rpt lpt1:
        1 file(s) copied
Q:\areaname.test>

Note that it finds the file in both cases, but cannot find the
printer unless the CD command is done with the 8.3 name.
LPT1 is a captured port, and is actually a printer on a server.

copy test.rpt \\beach1\reports

Works in both cases.  It also appears that both cases work
on a local printer.
```

UNIX so with the screen section.

Left Click).

In my project I have created a New Executable(avion, plane in English).
Here is my project.

AVION(1)
--avion.cbl
--avion.exe
----vol.rff
----vers.rff
----delete.rff
----create.rff

3)How is the avion.cbl structure program?
Is this correct?

       identification division.
       program-id. tp2.
       environment division.
       input-output section.
       file-control.
       file section.
       working-storage section.
       procedure division.

I want to limit the size of the text which can be entered in a TextBox.

I have another TextBox but I use it to enter a date (pic 99/99/9999).
5)Must I use a MaskEdit?

Thanks for your answer.
Sydney
